claim: Breaking daily workflows into domains, then tasks, then skills, then automations creates a systematic Claude Code architecture that can be tracked and optimized
actionable: Map your daily workflows into a domain->task->skill->automation hierarchy and codify each skill with prompts so Claude Code executes them consistently
source_title: Stop Using Claude Code Without an Agentic OS

claim: Claude Code's efficiency increases dramatically when given explicit process-driven skills rather than freestyle prompts because it has no memory between invocations
actionable: Create reusable skill files with detailed instructions and examples so Claude Code follows the exact same process each time you invoke a task
source_title: Stop Using Claude Code Without an Agentic OS

claim: Obsidian with a Karpathy-inspired RAG structure (raw -> wiki -> output folders) provides an effective memory layer for Claude Code without needing vector databases
actionable: Set up an Obsidian vault with raw/wiki/output subfolders to store inputs, wikified summaries, and final outputs from Claude Code automation runs
source_title: Stop Using Claude Code Without an Agentic OS

claim: A Claude.md file that describes the agentic OS structure is essential for Claude Code to understand memory layout and find information efficiently within the system
actionable: Create a detailed Claude.md file documenting your domain structure, task breakdown, and Obsidian folder hierarchy so Claude Code can navigate the system
source_title: Stop Using Claude Code Without an Agentic OS

claim: Local vs remote automations can be automatically determined by Claude Code based on the task, so developers don't need to manually decide deployment strategy
actionable: When creating an automation, tell Claude Code you want a local or remote automation and let it decide the implementation rather than manually planning the architecture
source_title: Stop Using Claude Code Without an Agentic OS

claim: Creating a visual dashboard that converts skills into clickable buttons allows non-technical team members and clients to execute automated workflows without using the terminal
actionable: Build a simple web dashboard that maps skills and automations to clickable buttons so clients can trigger workflows through UI instead of CLI
source_title: Stop Using Claude Code Without an Agentic OS

claim: Observability dashboards showing usage metrics, recent changes, and forecasts help track system performance and optimize Claude Code automation effectiveness over time
actionable: Create custom observability views in your dashboard showing token usage windows, routine triggers, and vault changes to monitor automation health
source_title: Stop Using Claude Code Without an Agentic OS

claim: The architecture (domain->task->skill->automation) is more valuable than the dashboard or observability layer because it codifies repeatable processes
actionable: Focus first on architecting your domain/task/skill breakdown before building dashboards or observability systems
source_title: Stop Using Claude Code Without an Agentic OS
